ddls:
CREATE TABLE "error_types" (
  "error_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error type */,
  "error_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the error type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the error type */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the error (e.g., critical, high, medium, low) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the error (e.g., network, application, database) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "app_version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  "owner" TEXT /* Owner of the application */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the application runs (e.g., production, staging) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric associated with this log */,
  "error_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of error encountered */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application related to this log */,
  "log_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Log level (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Log message */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log (e.g., API, database, frontend) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_error_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_type_id") REFERENCES error_types ("error_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicator if the user account is active */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "log_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user (e.g., country, city) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "setting_type" TEXT /* Type of setting (e.g., threshold, notification) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the setting */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicator if the setting is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric for which the prediction is made */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value of the metric */,
  "prediction_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the prediction was made */,
  "prediction_model" TEXT /* Machine learning model used for the prediction */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the prediction */,
  "prediction_status" TEXT /* Status of the prediction (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id")
)
